1.title: Desktop Computer
1.description: A large stationary computer used at home or in offices, with a separate screen, keyboard and mouse.

1.title: Laptop
1.description: A portable computer with a built-in screen and keyboard. Runs on battery so you can use it anywhere.

1.title: Smartphone
1.description: A pocket-sized computer you can also use to make phone calls, take photos, and browse the web.

2.title: CPU — The Brain
2.description: The Central Processing Unit does all the thinking and calculations. A faster CPU means a faster computer.

2.title: RAM — Short-term Memory
2.description: Random Access Memory holds what the computer is working on right now. More RAM = more tasks at once.

2.title: Hard Drive — Long-term Storage
2.description: Stores your files, photos, and programs even when the computer is turned off. SSDs are faster than HDDs.

3.title: Operating System
3.description: Windows, macOS, and Linux are operating systems. They manage everything the computer does and show the desktop.

3.title: Web Browser
3.description: Google Chrome, Firefox, and Edge are browsers. They let you visit websites and explore the internet.

3.title: Word Processor
3.description: Microsoft Word and Google Docs let you write letters, reports, and documents and save them as files.

4.title: Starting Up
4.description: Press the power button once and wait. The operating system loads — this usually takes 30 seconds to 2 minutes.

4.title: Shutting Down Properly
4.description: Always use Start > Shut Down. This saves your work and keeps files safe before turning off the power.

4.title: Restarting
4.description: Restarting clears problems and installs updates. If your computer is slow or frozen, try restarting first.

5.title: Left Click
5.description: Tap the left mouse button once to select something, and double-click to open a file or program.

5.title: Right Click
5.description: Tap the right mouse button to open a menu with extra options like Copy, Paste, and Properties.

5.title: Scroll Wheel
5.description: Roll the middle wheel up and down to scroll through long pages and documents without using the scroll bar.

6.title: Shift Key
6.description: Hold Shift while typing a letter to make it uppercase. Shift + a number key types special characters like ! @ #.

6.title: Shortcut Keys
6.description: Ctrl+C copies, Ctrl+V pastes, Ctrl+Z undoes. Learning shortcuts saves lots of time.

6.title: Enter Key
6.description: Press Enter to confirm something, start a new line when typing, or run a search.

7.title: Folders are like Drawers
7.description: Just like a filing cabinet has drawers, folders on a computer organise files into groups. You can make folders inside folders.

7.title: Files are Documents
7.description: A file is any item stored on a computer — a photo, a letter, a song, or a program. Files have names and types like .jpg or .pdf.

7.title: Finding Files
7.description: Use the search bar in File Explorer (Windows) to find a file by name. Searching is faster than clicking through folders.

8.title: Icons are Shortcuts
8.description: Every icon on the desktop is a shortcut to a program or file. Double-click any icon to open it.

8.title: Taskbar
8.description: The bar at the bottom of the screen shows open programs and quick-launch icons. Click an icon to switch between windows.

8.title: Recycle Bin
8.description: When you delete a file it goes to the Recycle Bin first. You can restore it or empty the bin to free up space.

9.title: World Wide Web
9.description: The web is a collection of billions of pages and files linked together. You access it through a web browser.

9.title: Wi-Fi & Broadband
9.description: Wi-Fi connects your device wirelessly to a router. The router connects to your internet service provider (ISP) which links to the world.

9.title: Links & URLs
9.description: Every website has a unique address called a URL (e.g. www.google.com). Links are clickable shortcuts to other pages.

10.title: Tabs
10.description: Open multiple websites at once using tabs at the top of the browser. Click a tab to switch between pages without losing your place.

10.title: Bookmarks
10.description: Save websites you visit often as bookmarks. Click the star icon in the address bar to bookmark a page.

10.title: Back & Refresh
10.description: The back arrow takes you to the previous page. The refresh button reloads the current page if it is slow or shows an error.

11.title: Using Keywords
11.description: Type short, specific words into a search engine. 'best free email app 2024' works better than 'what is a good email app to use please'.

11.title: Checking Sources
11.description: Not everything online is true. Look for well-known, trusted websites and check if multiple sources say the same thing.

11.title: Reading Results
11.description: The first results are often ads (marked 'Sponsored'). Scroll past them to find organic search results which are usually more reliable.

12.title: Composing an Email
12.description: Click New/Compose, type the recipient's address in the To: field, add a subject, write your message, then click Send.

12.title: Attachments
12.description: You can attach photos, documents, or files to an email by clicking the paperclip icon. Keep attachments under 25MB.

12.title: Inbox & Folders
12.description: Received emails go to your Inbox. Use folders like Spam, Sent, and Drafts to organise your messages.

13.title: Strong Passwords
13.description: Use at least 12 characters mixing letters, numbers and symbols. Never share your password and use a different one for each account.

13.title: Phishing Scams
13.description: Scammers send fake emails pretending to be banks or companies. Never click suspicious links or give personal details via email.

13.title: Antivirus Software
13.description: Keep antivirus software installed and updated. It scans for harmful programs and warns you before you download dangerous files.

14.title: Creating a Profile
14.description: Your profile includes a name, photo, and bio. Keep personal details like your address and phone number private.

14.title: Posting & Sharing
14.description: You can post text, photos, and videos for friends or the public to see. Think carefully before posting — it can be difficult to remove.

14.title: Online Etiquette
14.description: Be respectful in comments and messages. Cyberbullying is harmful — report and block accounts that behave badly.

15.title: Formatting Text
15.description: Use Bold, Italic, and Underline to emphasise words. Change font size and colour to make headings stand out.

15.title: Saving Your Work
15.description: Press Ctrl+S to save regularly. Use File > Save As to save a copy with a new name or in a different format like PDF.

15.title: Printing a Document
15.description: Go to File > Print (or Ctrl+P). Choose your printer, check the preview, and click Print. Save as PDF if you have no printer.

16.title: Logging In Safely
16.description: Always type your bank's URL directly — never click email links. Use your official banking app on your phone for extra security.

16.title: Two-Factor Authentication
16.description: Banks send a one-time code to your phone to confirm it is really you. Always enable this extra security layer.

16.title: Spotting Fraud
16.description: Banks never ask for your full password or PIN online or by phone. If something feels wrong, call your bank directly.

17.title: Taking Good Photos
17.description: Hold the camera steady, make sure there is good light, and tap the screen to focus before shooting.

17.title: Organising Your Gallery
17.description: Create albums by date or topic (Holidays, Family) to keep thousands of photos easy to find.

17.title: Backing Up Photos
17.description: Enable auto-backup to Google Photos or iCloud so your pictures are safe even if your phone is lost or broken.

18.title: Streaming Video
18.description: Netflix, YouTube, and Disney+ let you watch films and shows on demand. You need a fast internet connection to stream in HD.

18.title: Music Streaming
18.description: Spotify and Apple Music give you millions of songs. Download playlists for offline listening when you have no signal.

18.title: Video Calls
18.description: Zoom, Teams, and WhatsApp let you see and talk to people in real time. You need a camera and microphone.

19.title: Disk Cleanup
19.description: Use Disk Cleanup (Windows) or Storage Management (Mac) to delete temporary files and free up space on your hard drive.

19.title: Software Updates
19.description: Install updates for your operating system and apps regularly. Updates fix security holes and improve performance.

19.title: Keeping Cool
19.description: Keep vents unblocked and clean dust from fans. Overheating slows computers down and can cause permanent damage.

20.title: Try Restarting First
20.description: Restarting fixes most common problems — slow performance, frozen programs, and network issues. Always try this first.

20.title: Search for the Error
20.description: Copy the exact error message and paste it into Google. Millions of people have had the same problem and found solutions.

20.title: Ask for Help
20.description: Contact your internet provider for connection issues, your computer manufacturer for hardware faults, or a local tech shop.

21.title: Google Drive
21.description: Free storage for photos, documents, and files. Access from any device. 15GB free with a Google account.

21.title: iCloud
21.description: Apple's cloud service automatically backs up iPhones, iPads, and Macs. 5GB free, easy to upgrade.

21.title: OneDrive
21.description: Built into Windows. Automatically syncs your Desktop and Documents folders so they are always backed up.

22.title: Smartphone Apps
22.description: Apps are small programs. The App Store (Apple) and Google Play Store (Android) have millions of free and paid apps.

22.title: Mobile Data
22.description: Your phone connects to the internet using Wi-Fi or mobile data (4G/5G). Use Wi-Fi when available to save your data allowance.

22.title: Battery & Storage
22.description: Close unused apps to save battery. Delete old photos and apps you no longer use to free up storage space.

23.title: Respect Online
23.description: Treat people with the same kindness online as you would in person. Words can hurt even through a screen.

23.title: Protect Your Privacy
23.description: Think before you share. Personal information like your address, school, or daily routine should stay private online.

23.title: Verify Before Sharing
23.description: Misinformation spreads fast. Check facts with trusted news sources before sharing posts, photos, or videos.
